title: US and Iran trade strikes, threatening fragile truce
subhead: Iran's central military command accused the US of violating the ceasefire with attacks in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, saying they struck US forces in retaliation. The US says Iran attacked three American destroyers first, which Trump later downplayed and said he considered the ceasefire still active.

title: Ghanaian author Nana Darkoa Sekyiamah challenges stereotypes about African women's sexuality
subhead: In her book 'The Sex Lives of African Women,' Sekyiamah, a well-known figure in Ghanaian and African feminist circles, depicts the intimate lives of 31 women from the continent and the diaspora.

title: After PSG qualifies for the Champions League final, fans face travel headaches getting to Budapest
subhead: Paris Saint-Germain supporters hoping to attend the match against Arsenal on May 30 in the Hungarian capital must face high prices and the club's very selective ticket allocation process.

title: How sanctioned doctors kept practicing across the French-Belgian border
subhead: Because they fail to consult European alerts on suspensions or removals, France and Belgium allow doctors with problematic pasts to practice in their neighboring country.

title: Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'The United States has lost its moral authority for good'
subhead: The United States is turning its back on democracy and freedom – values the nation once embodied – and is now replacing them with the law of the strongest and a culture of deal-making.
